codeReview.summary: The fix commit does add a polling-safe session bootstrap helper and updates workspace-switch polls to use it, which addresses the reported transient 500 failure mode. But the same commit also broadens VAL-CROSS-005 into a running-timer continuity scenario with new setup, assertions, and cleanup that is outside the scoped flake fix and was split into its own follow-up commit immediately afterward.

issues.description: This feature was scoped to stabilizing readSessionBootstrap polling after workspace switch, but commit 94f7496 also rewrote VAL-CROSS-005 into a broader running-timer continuity scenario with extra project setup, timer start/stop flow, and current-timer identity assertions. The frontend-test-worker skill requires flaky-fix work to stay narrowly scoped, and git history shows this broader proof was subsequently isolated in commit a7b08e0, so the reviewed commit is not cleanly limited to the assigned session-bootstrap stabilization.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff marked skill procedure as followed even though the worker skipped the skill's required manual browser verification path without recording a blocking reason or exception.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/frontend-test-worker/SKILL.md step 9 requires agent-browser verification or a documented exception; workerSessionId 73a1e049-5714-415f-ab21-fd3577c7ab24 recorded no interactive checks in the handoff and the transcript skeleton shows no browser-tool attempt.

summary: Fail: commit 94f7496 contains the intended pollSessionBootstrap stabilization, but it also bundles an unrelated VAL-CROSS-005 running-timer test expansion, so the fix review still fails on scope discipline and traceability.
